summary: DHH announces that Evan Williams' new venture Odeo, a portal for podcasting, is built on Ruby on Rails. He highlights that Williams (of Blogger fame) hired Rabble as lead developer after advertising for a Rails developer in December 2004. DHH expresses excitement both about the podcasting product itself and about another high-profile site choosing Rails. The post serves as early Rails evangelism, using Odeo's launch as validation of the framework's viability.
key_points: Evan Williams' new startup Odeo aims to make it easy to discover, create, and subscribe to podcast content
Odeo was built on Ruby on Rails after Williams advertised for a Rails developer in late 2004
The launch attracted New York Times coverage, signaling mainstream interest in podcasting
DHH frames Odeo as validation that high-profile ventures are choosing Rails
money_quotes: It's going to make '...it easy for you to discover, create, and subscribe to fresh, independent audio content for your iPod'.
What also makes me excited is Odeo running Ruby on Rails.
It's great to see another high-profile site launch on Rails and loving it. Keep 'em coming.
key_insight: In the early days of Rails, DHH leveraged every high-profile adoption — like Evan Williams choosing Rails for Odeo — as proof that the framework was ready for serious, venture-backed products.
